rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY MARQUEZ VS. PAC OPERATING LIMITED PARTNERSHIP
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant PAC Operating Limited Partnership's demurrer: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KEAYS ET AL VS. 50 BEALE INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained on the ground that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. It is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, by identifying the product(s) for which defendant is allegedly responsible. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
